title: Geas Task Contract
description: Canonical task contract capturing task intent, routing, acceptance criteria, approval state, baseline, and lifecycle status.

task_id.description: Stable task identifier within a mission.

title.description: Short human-readable label for the task.

goal.description: Concrete outcome the task is expected to achieve.

risk_level.description: Failure-cost classification used for routing and review conservatism.

acceptance_criteria.description: Observable criteria that must hold before the task may be closed as complete.

verification_plan.description: Description of how this task will be verified. Markdown allowed. Covers both automated and manual verification approaches in prose.

approved_by.description: Who approved this task contract as ready to enter execution. null means not yet approved. 'user' is required for the initial task set and for any task outside the current mission scope. 'decision_maker' is allowed for mid-mission tasks added within the current mission scope.

surfaces.description: Reviewer-readable impact surfaces touched by the task.

primary_worker_type.description: Concrete implementation-specific worker type. Primary worker is always the implementer slot; this field records which concrete implementer handles the task.

required_reviewer_slots.description: Final reviewer slots chosen by the orchestrator after considering risk, surfaces, and mission context.

required_reviewer_types.description: Concrete reviewer types resolved from required_reviewer_slots by the implementation.

base_snapshot.description: Shared baseline snapshot this task starts from. Format is implementation-specific.

dependencies.description: Task IDs that must reach 'passed' before this task can start. Empty array if no dependencies.

supersedes.description: Task id that this contract replaces. Set when this task is created to take over work from a cancelled predecessor. Null when this task is not a replacement.

status.description: Current lifecycle status for the task and its execution.
